novel_features.name: Per-food CSV export
novel_features.description: Export your food diary to CSV with one row per logged food, not per meal. Premium MFP only ships per-meal CSVs.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the local diary_entry store; MFP's own CSV gates per-food granularity behind a tier that still doesn't deliver it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs the user's full eating history at food granularity for analysis, training, or long-form coaching memory.

novel_features.name: Weight-trend vs deficit
novel_features.description: Computes weekly weight slope, weekly average calorie deficit, and the implied calories-per-pound ratio.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining weight measurements, daily calorie totals, and goal snapshots locally — no single MFP endpoint returns it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent is helping the user calibrate a deficit or surplus and needs to know whether the plan is actually moving the scale.

novel_features.name: Agent context dump
novel_features.description: Single-call snapshot of the last N days: diary totals, weight trend, current goals, recent foods, macro deltas — sized for an agent context window.
novel_features.rationale: Composes 5+ tables of local state into one JSON; AdamWalt MCP requires N tool calls for the same picture.
novel_features.why_it_matters: First call any agent should make before reasoning about a user's nutrition — gives the full picture in one shot.

novel_features.name: Top foods by nutrient driver
novel_features.description: Pareto query: which N foods drove X% of your protein/carbs/fat/fiber/sugar/calories over a window?
novel_features.rationale: Requires SQL group-by over diary_entry rows in local SQLite; no API endpoint exposes this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent is helping the user understand what's actually driving a macro target, not what they think is.

novel_features.name: Macro-trend report
novel_features.description: Daily protein/carbs/fat/fiber over a date range, with optional 7-day smoothing. Reads canonical reports endpoint, fills gaps from local diary.
novel_features.rationale: Combines the canonical /api/services/reports/results JSON island with local fallback — no wrapper orchestrates multi-nutrient.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to detect macro drift over weeks without rescraping the whole diary.

novel_features.name: Week-vs-week diff
novel_features.description: Side-by-side: avg calories, macros, weight delta, top-3 foods that changed contribution between two recent weeks.
novel_features.rationale: Two windowed aggregates over diary_entry, measurement, and goal_snapshot in local SQLite.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the user asks 'how did this week compare to last' — the agent answers with one call instead of two diary fetches.

novel_features.name: Find food in diary (FTS)
novel_features.description: Full-text search across every diary entry and food in the local store. Returns date, meal, servings, calories per match.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local FTS5 indexes (diary_entries_fts + foods_fts); MFP web has no recall search.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to recall every time the user logged a specific food without scrolling through months of diary.

novel_features.name: Sync diff
novel_features.description: After a sync, prints what changed: new diary entries, edited entries, new measurements, new water logs since the last sync.
novel_features.rationale: Local sync timestamps and row diffs — no external API call needed.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use in cron pipelines to surface only the new data to downstream tools (Hugo summaries, Sheets exports).

novel_features.name: Reports backfill
novel_features.description: Fans out over the JSON reports endpoint to cache 2 years × N nutrients into the local report_snapshot table in one command.
novel_features.rationale: MFP's reports endpoint is a hidden JSON island; no wrapper persists it. The free tier only retains 2 years; backfill captures the full history before it expires.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run once after install to give every analytics command a 2-year history without per-query rescraping.

novel_features.name: Macro-gap candidate foods
novel_features.description: Lists foods from your favorites and last-30-day recents whose servings would fill the remaining macro envelope for today.
novel_features.rationale: Joins food + diary_entry recency in local SQLite with the user's macro gap; reframed away from any LLM dependency.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pipe to `| claude "pick one"` to fold in language reasoning without coupling the CLI to an LLM.

novel_features.name: Adherence streak
novel_features.description: Longest run of consecutive days where calorie totals fall within ±tolerance of your goal.
novel_features.rationale: Mechanical streak query over diary_entry and goal_snapshot; motivational primitive no MFP screen exposes directly.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the user asks how their adherence is trending — answer arrives without subjective interpretation.

novel_features.name: Saved-meal expansion
novel_features.description: Dereferences a saved meal into per-food rows with full nutrients, instead of MFP's collapsed display.
novel_features.rationale: Same gated-content pattern as per-food CSV — MFP shows the rolled-up meal but hides the components.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when copying a saved meal's exact composition to another tracking system or analyzing what the user actually eats inside their named meals.

novel_features_built.name: Per-food CSV export
novel_features_built.description: Export your food diary to CSV with one row per logged food, not per meal. Premium MFP only ships per-meal CSVs.
novel_features_built.rationale: Requires the local diary_entry store; MFP's own CSV gates per-food granularity behind a tier that still doesn't deliver it.
novel_features_built.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs the user's full eating history at food granularity for analysis, training, or long-form coaching memory.

narrative.headline: Pull every meal you ever logged out of MyFitnessPal — per-food CSV, agent-shaped trends, and a local SQLite store no other MFP tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: MyFitnessPal closed their API, gates per-food export behind premium, and ships per-meal rows even there. This CLI imports your browser session, syncs your diary to local SQLite, and answers questions the official UI never can: which 5 foods drove 80% of your protein this quarter, what's your weekly weight slope vs your deficit, what changed since last sync. Built with agent-native output (`--json`, `--select`, `context`) so Claude can reason over your last 14 days in one tool call.
narrative.auth_narrative: MyFitnessPal closed their public API. This CLI uses your logged-in browser session — log in to myfitnesspal.com in Chrome, then run `myfitnesspal-pp-cli auth login --chrome`. Cookies are read from the .myfitnesspal.com domain. Sessions usually last 7-30 days; when they expire, log in again in Chrome and re-run `auth login --chrome`.

quickstart.comment: Imports your MFP cookies from Chrome (one-time setup; works after you've logged in to myfitnesspal.com)
Verifies the session is valid and api.myfitnesspal.com is reachable
Pulls four months of diary, exercises, water, measurements, and goals into the local SQLite store
Per-food CSV export — the headline thing premium MFP doesn't deliver
One-shot agent context: 14 days of diary totals, weight trend, current goals, recent foods, macro deltas
Joins weight measurements with calorie deficit to compute the implied calories-per-pound ratio

troubleshoots.fix: Log in to myfitnesspal.com in Chrome (the same profile you used for `auth login --chrome`), then re-run `myfitnesspal-pp-cli auth login --chrome`. Sessions typically last 7-30 days.

Install one of: `pip install pycookiecheat`, `cargo install cookie-scoop-cli`, or `brew install cookies`. Then re-run `auth login --chrome --profile Default`. If you have multiple Chrome profiles, list them with `auth login --chrome --list-profiles`.
Some food ids are user-scoped (only readable by accounts that have logged that food). Try `food search --query <name>` first to get an accessible food id.
Session cookie expired. Re-run `auth login --chrome` after logging in to myfitnesspal.com again.
MFP throttles bursty access. The CLI defaults to 1 req/sec; if you raised --concurrency, drop it back to 1 and retry. Wait 60 seconds before re-running.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI when an agent needs to reason over a user's actual eating, exercise, or weight history — especially across windows longer than today. The local SQLite store + agent-shaped `context` command means one tool call replaces the back-and-forth of pasting yesterday's diary into the chat. Use it for nutrition coaching, deficit calibration, macro analysis, recall questions ('every time I logged X'), and anywhere the official MFP UI's per-meal rollup blocks the question.

recipes.title: Build agent context for a coaching question
recipes.explanation: One tool call gives the agent everything it needs to answer 'should I cut more this week?' without back-and-forth pasting.

recipes.title: Find every time you logged a food
recipes.explanation: Local FTS over your synced diary; instant answer to recall questions the MFP web UI requires scrolling for.

recipes.title: Per-food CSV for a quarter
recipes.explanation: One row per food entry with full nutrients — the export premium MFP doesn't deliver.

recipes.title: Weekly diff for the Sunday review
recipes.explanation: Side-by-side calories, macros, weight delta, and the top-3 foods that changed contribution between this week and last.

recipes.title: Calibrate a deficit
recipes.explanation: Returns weekly weight slope, weekly average deficit, and the implied calories-per-pound — the calculation Priya runs in a Google Sheet today.

trigger_phrases: what did I eat this week
